summary: This week's AI news highlights significant developments in the industry, including Apple's lawsuit against OpenAI, advancements in AI-powered hardware, and growing concerns about AI misuse. Key players like Google, NVIDIA, and Databricks are making strides in AI research and applications.
insight: The increasing focus on AI regulation and ethics underscores the need for responsible AI development and deployment, as seen in Apple's lawsuit against OpenAI and the EU's order for Google to open Android and Search to rivals.
highlights: Apple's lawsuit against OpenAI could disrupt the latter's IPO plans
Databricks hits $188B valuation, extending its run as AI's favorite second act
Google ordered to open Android and Search to rivals in Europe

articles.title: Can an Apple lawsuit derail OpenAI’s hardware plans?
articles.description: On the latest episode of Equity, we debate whether Apple's lawsuit will cast over OpenAi's much-discussed plans to get into hardware and go public.

articles.title: Databricks hits $188B valuation, extending its run as AI’s favorite second act
articles.description: Databricks has remade its image into an AI company and has published research on the cost savings of open weight AI models for coding.

articles.title: How Apple’s big lawsuit could disrupt OpenAI’s IPO plans
articles.description: Apple filed a trade secrets lawsuit against OpenAI last Friday, and it’s not messing around. The complaint alleges a pattern of misconduct reaching all the way up to OpenAI’s chief

articles.title: It's official: EU will force Google to share search data and open up AI on Android
articles.description: Google says these changes could endanger user privacy and security.

articles.title: Google ordered to open Android and Search to rivals in Europe
articles.description: Google must give rival AI assistants and search engines greater access to key parts of Android and Google Search after the European Union ordered the company to comply with the blo

articles.title: What to watch for after Jensen Huang’s Japan visit
articles.description: Jensen Huang left Tokyo with deals spanning Japan's entire tech ecosystem.

articles.title: Vertu wants executives to pay $6,880 for an AI agent — here’s how it actually performs
articles.description: From AI workflows to battery life and security, here's what it's really like to live with Vertu's luxury foldable every day.

articles.title: Agility Robotics plants its flag in Tesla’s backyard
articles.description: Agility is opening a new training center for its Digit robots in Fremont, California.

articles.title: Fear of humanoid robots spurs human workers to strike at Hyundai auto factory
articles.description: Hyundai aims to deploy 25,000 Atlas robots starting with US factories in 2028.

articles.title: Nonprofit Current AI is racing to build the World Wide Web of AI, free for all
articles.description: Current AI, a non-profit building AI that leaves no one culture behind, has made remarkable progress across devices, AI chat and more.

articles.title: Fine-tune video and image models at scale with NVIDIA NeMo Automodel and 🤗 Diffusers
Examining Google DeepMind’s AI bioresilience push
